mappings.rationaleAI: Foundational chemistry provides context for inorganic milieu (A).
Thermodynamics connects to inorganic milieu (A).
Biomolecules address organic chemicals (B) including carbohydrates (B.1), lipids (B.2), proteins (B.3), ATP (B.4), nucleic acids (B.5), and enzymes (B.7).
Biological catalysts address enzymes (B.7).
Endocrine physiology addresses hormones (B.9) including general features (B.9.a) and vertebrate hormones (B.9.b).
Molecular structures connect to organic chemicals (B) including proteins (B.3) and nucleic acids (B.5).
Carbon compounds connect to organic chemicals (B) and natural products (B.10).
DNA and proteins address B.3, nucleic acids (B.5), and drug development (C).
Pharmacology addresses drugs (C) comprehensively including sources, action, metabolism, organ effects, antibiotics, cancer drugs, addiction, and alcohol.
Nutrients connect to carbohydrates (B.1), lipids (B.2), proteins (B.3), and vitamins (B.8).
Substance addiction addresses drug use (C.7) and alcohol (D).
Immunotherapy connects to immune response drugs (C.4.h).
Antibiotic resistance connects to disease drugs (C.5) and biotoxins (E.3).
Cancer treatment addresses cancer drugs (C.6).
Antibiotic resistance connects to antibiotics (C.5.a) and toxins (E.3).
Anaesthetic drugs connect to drug action (C.2), metabolism (C.3), and CNS drugs (C.4.c).
Circadian-hormonal interplay connects to hormones (B.9).
DNA connects to nucleic acids (B.5, B.5.b).
Covers plant biology including pollination mechanisms and the role of plants in medicines and agriculture, relevant to hormones of plants and biocides directed against plant pests.

Examines insect-microbe partnerships and horizontal gene transfer relevant to enzymes and biological processes, and covers biotoxins and microbial interactions relevant to toxins produced by microorganisms.

Covers fungi as agents of crop disease causing historical famines (biocides against plant pests) and fungal biology including parasitism and decomposition relevant to biotoxins produced by microorganisms.

Covers antiretroviral drug development and drug resistance mechanisms, directly addressing chemotherapeutic drugs used to treat infectious diseases and drug use patterns in the context of a global epidemic.

Traces the development of organic chemistry and chemical synthesis, relevant to understanding the sources and development of drugs and the chemical foundations of organic compounds participating in life processes.

Covers physiological mechanisms including homeostasis, immune defense, and reproductive physiology, relevant to hormones and their regulation and to the classification of drugs by organ system of principal effect.
Covers agricultural practice including pest management, genetically modified organisms, and sustainability, directly relevant to biocides directed against animal and plant pests.
Covers patterning by Hox genes, signaling molecules like Sonic hedgehog, and the role of chemical signaling during growth, directly relevant to hormones and nucleic acids in vital processes.
Covers cell biology including signaling, differentiation, and programmed cell death involving proteins, nucleic acids, and enzymes as fundamental chemicals in vital processes.
